sp_widget.description: Custom unauthenticated Service Portal widget for case status lookup. Renders a single case-number input field plus a Look Up Status button, GETs /api/x_casemgmt/case_status_lookup, and displays ONLY the whitelisted fields { status, subject, opened_date } on match or the verbatim text 'No case found with that number.' on no-match per AAP Section 0.7.4. Embedded by sp_page_x_casemgmt_case_status. No internal fields (assigned_group, assigned_agent, description, closed_date, requester_name, requester_email, priority, type, pending_reason, audit fields) are exposed. All HTTP errors (404, 500, 400, network) render the same verbatim message for information-leakage hardening. Replaces ArkCase's GetCaseByNumberServiceImpl.getCaseByNumber() and PortalRequestService.getRequestStatus() Spring services. No client-side or server-side database operations - all data flows through the scripted REST endpoint. Per AAP Sections 0.4.3, 0.4.4, 0.5.1, and 0.7.4 (verbatim no-case-found message) and Validation Gate 5 (Portal - lookup).
sp_widget.name: Case Lookup Widget
sp_widget.script: (function() { /* * Case Lookup Widget - server-side data initialization. * * No server-side data fetch is needed. The case-status lookup is * driven entirely by the client-side controller via $http.get to * the scripted REST endpoint at * /api/x_casemgmt/case_status_lookup?number=<encoded> * defined by ../rest/sys_ws_definition_x_casemgmt_case_status_lookup.xml * and handled by ../rest/sys_ws_operation_x_casemgmt_case_status_lookup_get.xml. * * This widget intentionally performs ZERO database operations: * - No GlideRecord reads (no pre-fetching of any case data; the * widget renders with empty state and the user drives the * lookup via the form). * - No GlideRecord writes (the lookup is read-only by definition; * all writes in this scoped application go through the * scripted REST endpoints, never through widget server scripts). * - No gs.eventQueue() calls (email notifications are disabled * per AAP Section 0.7.2 Email-disabled constraint). * * The IIFE pattern matches ServiceNow's standard widget server- * script convention; the empty body is the canonical no-op * placeholder. */ })();
sp_widget.template:
Case Status Lookup

Case Status

Status
{{c.result.status}}
Subject
{{c.result.subject}}
Opened Date
{{c.result.opened_date}}
No case found with that number.
